texts.label: text
texts.orig: Super flat to choppy water; Lagoon; Sandbanks; Waves at the reef (Up To 2.5m in Kuzi Season only) // Tidal: Yes
texts.text: Super flat to choppy water; Lagoon; Sandbanks; Waves at the reef (Up To 2.5m in Kuzi Season only) // Tidal: Yes

texts.label: text
texts.orig: DRIVE from: Mombasa (MBA) 2.5H // Malindi (MYD) 30min FLIGHT from: Nairobi (NBO) 1H
texts.text: DRIVE from: Mombasa (MBA) 2.5H // Malindi (MYD) 30min FLIGHT from: Nairobi (NBO) 1H

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Kaskazi Season // 15 - 25 knots // December - April // Cross on-shore Kuzi Season // 20 - 35 knots // June - September // Cross on-shore
texts.text: Kaskazi Season // 15 - 25 knots // December - April // Cross on-shore Kuzi Season // 20 - 35 knots // June - September // Cross on-shore

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Water temperature // 25-30°C / 77°F - 86°F all year round. The air temperature can reach 36°C / 97°F during the Kaskazi season. Kuzi is a little cooler at about 28°C / 82°F.
texts.text: Water temperature // 25-30°C / 77°F - 86°F all year round. The air temperature can reach 36°C / 97°F during the Kaskazi season. Kuzi is a little cooler at about 28°C / 82°F.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The windy season is split into two, providing trade winds from December to April, known as the Kaskhazi, and Kuzi, which is after the few rainy months starts from May and stays until September. During the day the wind tends to be lighter in the morning as and gradually increase until about midday and stays strong until the evening.
texts.text: The windy season is split into two, providing trade winds from December to April, known as the Kaskhazi, and Kuzi, which is after the few rainy months starts from May and stays until September. During the day the wind tends to be lighter in the morning as and gradually increase until about midday and stays strong until the evening.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Kaskhazi blows from the North/North East (cross-onshore from the left) at around 15-25 knots (mostly around 17 knots), increasing in the afternoon with flat water and a little chop. Most used kites sizes are between 10 - 15 meters.
texts.text: The Kaskhazi blows from the North/North East (cross-onshore from the left) at around 15-25 knots (mostly around 17 knots), increasing in the afternoon with flat water and a little chop. Most used kites sizes are between 10 - 15 meters.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The Kuzi blows from the South/Southeast (cross-onshore from the right) and with the stronger wind days can bring the heavy chop with swells, The Kuzi is the stronger of the two and blows most days between 17-32 knots but averages around 20 knots. Calmer in the morning and gets typically stronger after lunch. Most used kites sizes are between 6 - 12 meters.
texts.text: The Kuzi blows from the South/Southeast (cross-onshore from the right) and with the stronger wind days can bring the heavy chop with swells, The Kuzi is the stronger of the two and blows most days between 17-32 knots but averages around 20 knots. Calmer in the morning and gets typically stronger after lunch. Most used kites sizes are between 6 - 12 meters.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Watamu is a small and peaceful fishing village located on the Kenyan coast about 110 km northeast of Mombasa and 15 km south of Malindi. It is a designated UN area of outstanding natural beauty and protected natural reserves. Our coastline spreads across 5 kilometers of pristine white sand beaches and lush tropical forests.
texts.text: Watamu is a small and peaceful fishing village located on the Kenyan coast about 110 km northeast of Mombasa and 15 km south of Malindi. It is a designated UN area of outstanding natural beauty and protected natural reserves. Our coastline spreads across 5 kilometers of pristine white sand beaches and lush tropical forests.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This small peninsula within the Mida Creek is surrounded by bays and lagoons separated by coral reefs and  exposed  sandbanks.  Constant  trade  winds  provide  a  variety  of  water  conditions  that  every kitesurfer  will  enjoy.  The  surrounding  attractions  and  world-class  game  reserves  set  Kenya  to  be  a unique  all-around  kitesurfing  holiday  destination  and  will  provide  tons  of  activities  on  lighter  wind days.
texts.text: This small peninsula within the Mida Creek is surrounded by bays and lagoons separated by coral reefs and  exposed  sandbanks.  Constant  trade  winds  provide  a  variety  of  water  conditions  that  every kitesurfer  will  enjoy.  The  surrounding  attractions  and  world-class  game  reserves  set  Kenya  to  be  a unique  all-around  kitesurfing  holiday  destination  and  will  provide  tons  of  activities  on  lighter  wind days.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The village has a few supermarkets for groceries and plenty of local vendors offering fresh fruit, local drinks, local cuisine as well as jewelry, shoes, and other local crafts. There also is a distinct Italian influence in the area. So you will find many pizza restaurants and more contemporary dining options outside  of  the  hotel.  For  nightlife/going-out  you'll  find  our  famous  LichtHaus  bar  where  most Watamu travelers  come  to  meet  and  greet  each  other.  It  is  also  the  best  sunset  spot  and  hosts regular parties. But there are also other bars and places in the area along the way to the Watamu center.
texts.text: The village has a few supermarkets for groceries and plenty of local vendors offering fresh fruit, local drinks, local cuisine as well as jewelry, shoes, and other local crafts. There also is a distinct Italian influence in the area. So you will find many pizza restaurants and more contemporary dining options outside  of  the  hotel.  For  nightlife/going-out  you'll  find  our  famous  LichtHaus  bar  where  most Watamu travelers  come  to  meet  and  greet  each  other.  It  is  also  the  best  sunset  spot  and  hosts regular parties. But there are also other bars and places in the area along the way to the Watamu center.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The major attractions, in addition to the Marine National Park, are the mangrove forest of Mida Creek, the ruins of Gede, and the Arabuko Sokoke forest. And only two hours from Watamu you can also explore the Marafa Hell's Kitchen Canyon and the Tsavo East National Safari Park.
texts.text: The major attractions, in addition to the Marine National Park, are the mangrove forest of Mida Creek, the ruins of Gede, and the Arabuko Sokoke forest. And only two hours from Watamu you can also explore the Marafa Hell's Kitchen Canyon and the Tsavo East National Safari Park.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The most popular of the kitesurfing spots in Watamu is long beach. It stretches 5 km along the coast. The water is mostly flat with with few waves and patches of coral reef. This setup works really well especially during low tides. The wind blows consistently cross on-shore in both seasons.
texts.text: The most popular of the kitesurfing spots in Watamu is long beach. It stretches 5 km along the coast. The water is mostly flat with with few waves and patches of coral reef. This setup works really well especially during low tides. The wind blows consistently cross on-shore in both seasons.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Explore beautiful turquoise water with amazing wildlife around. The best time to rock up would be on low tide as the water is nice and flat. But watch out for tourists: it can get a bit busier on the sandbank. Again, consistent cross on-shore winds blow all year round.
texts.text: Explore beautiful turquoise water with amazing wildlife around. The best time to rock up would be on low tide as the water is nice and flat. But watch out for tourists: it can get a bit busier on the sandbank. Again, consistent cross on-shore winds blow all year round.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This spot is a little bit of a trip from Watamu due to being a bit hidden up the coast. Silversands tends to work way better in Kuzi season. That's from June till September when the wind hits this spot just slightly cross off-shore which also delivers some awesome waves. So if you enjoy wave riding, make sure you cross this one of your list.
texts.text: This spot is a little bit of a trip from Watamu due to being a bit hidden up the coast. Silversands tends to work way better in Kuzi season. That's from June till September when the wind hits this spot just slightly cross off-shore which also delivers some awesome waves. So if you enjoy wave riding, make sure you cross this one of your list.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Jacaranda is considered as one of the world's top flat water kite spots. This spot is the best on neap tide and works great in both Kaskazi and Kuzi wind seasons with consistent cross on-shore wind. It's about 20 minutes North of the hotel, so there is no excuse not to go!
texts.text: Jacaranda is considered as one of the world's top flat water kite spots. This spot is the best on neap tide and works great in both Kaskazi and Kuzi wind seasons with consistent cross on-shore wind. It's about 20 minutes North of the hotel, so there is no excuse not to go!

texts.label: text
texts.orig: One of our favourites, however slightly tricky. This spot works only during the Kuzi season (June -  September) and has a few things such as strong tides, hidden coral stones and the occasional sea urchin to watch out for. However, during sunset it creates the feeling of pure freedom. It just needs to be done right and not alone. For details please get in touch, and we'll set you up.
texts.text: One of our favourites, however slightly tricky. This spot works only during the Kuzi season (June -  September) and has a few things such as strong tides, hidden coral stones and the occasional sea urchin to watch out for. However, during sunset it creates the feeling of pure freedom. It just needs to be done right and not alone. For details please get in touch, and we'll set you up.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Kite Spot - Watamu Silversands Kite Map Jacaranda Kite Beach Creek Entrance Sandbank
texts.text: Kite Spot - Watamu Silversands Kite Map Jacaranda Kite Beach Creek Entrance Sandbank

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Watamu area stretch across kilometres of white sand beaches providing crystal clear and warm water all year round. The reefs' surrounding lagoon, 300-500 m away from the shore, forms excellent small kickers and when the summer swell is right - some waves (2-3m) at the reef. Depending on the state of  the  tide  you  can  expect  flat  to  slightly  choppy  water  conditions.  All  along  the  coast  some  large sandbars inside of this lagoon creates the flat water oasis for all level kitesurfers seeking to improve their skills.
texts.text: Watamu area stretch across kilometres of white sand beaches providing crystal clear and warm water all year round. The reefs' surrounding lagoon, 300-500 m away from the shore, forms excellent small kickers and when the summer swell is right - some waves (2-3m) at the reef. Depending on the state of  the  tide  you  can  expect  flat  to  slightly  choppy  water  conditions.  All  along  the  coast  some  large sandbars inside of this lagoon creates the flat water oasis for all level kitesurfers seeking to improve their skills.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Main kitesurfing spots can be found south of the Watamu Village where the kitesurfing schools are located. Ideal for beginners or kitesurfers who need to rent their gear or want to be surrounded by other likeminded people. If you are an independent rider and want to run away from the crowds (not that there are many as the lagoon is just enormous), you can find many spots between Malindi and Watamu where you can enjoy your kitesurfing paradise with no one else around.
texts.text: Main kitesurfing spots can be found south of the Watamu Village where the kitesurfing schools are located. Ideal for beginners or kitesurfers who need to rent their gear or want to be surrounded by other likeminded people. If you are an independent rider and want to run away from the crowds (not that there are many as the lagoon is just enormous), you can find many spots between Malindi and Watamu where you can enjoy your kitesurfing paradise with no one else around.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: This location offers perfect onshore - cross-onshore winds all year round with only a few rainy months between the two windy seasons. In addition to that, some of the areas in the lagoon are much deeper providing ridable condition at any states of the tide (except for extreme spring tide).
texts.text: This location offers perfect onshore - cross-onshore winds all year round with only a few rainy months between the two windy seasons. In addition to that, some of the areas in the lagoon are much deeper providing ridable condition at any states of the tide (except for extreme spring tide).

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: Food & Drink on the kite spots:
texts.text: Food & Drink on the kite spots:

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Some kite spots have restaurants and bars and some don't. But don't you worry we got you covered. We make sure you always get cold drinks, fruit, fresh coconut, and snacks no matter where you kite.
texts.text: Some kite spots have restaurants and bars and some don't. But don't you worry we got you covered. We make sure you always get cold drinks, fruit, fresh coconut, and snacks no matter where you kite.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: We got kite clothes, reef-save sun protection and zinc sticks as well as kite parts and repair service options. And our two partners sell Duotone and Slingshot gear in their shops.
texts.text: We got kite clothes, reef-save sun protection and zinc sticks as well as kite parts and repair service options. And our two partners sell Duotone and Slingshot gear in their shops.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Downwinders hold a special place  in the hearts  of riders who've been on them.
texts.text: Downwinders hold a special place  in the hearts  of riders who've been on them.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: The excitement and achievement that comes from starting  in  one  spot  and  kiting  to  another  is  infectious. Nothing quite captures the true essence of the freedom kiteboarding brings than jumping on a board and letting the wind carry you to your next port of call.
texts.text: The excitement and achievement that comes from starting  in  one  spot  and  kiting  to  another  is  infectious. Nothing quite captures the true essence of the freedom kiteboarding brings than jumping on a board and letting the wind carry you to your next port of call.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Sounds good doesn't it? If you're thinking of embarking on  your  first  downwind  adventure  then  we  have  you sorted.
texts.text: Sounds good doesn't it? If you're thinking of embarking on  your  first  downwind  adventure  then  we  have  you sorted.

texts.label: text
texts.orig: 15KM of free ride, approx. 2,5 hours (Includes: sunset drinks & snacks on arrival at the beach)
texts.text: 15KM of free ride, approx. 2,5 hours (Includes: sunset drinks & snacks on arrival at the beach)

texts.label: text
texts.orig: Are you ready for the ultimate kitesurfing experience?
texts.text: Are you ready for the ultimate kitesurfing experience?

texts.label: text
texts.orig: After breakfast, we transport you via boat to the kite spot where all of your gear will already be pumped and rigged by our team-within minutes you'll be on the water, kiting in your private paradise of glassy turquoise water. The location is all sand-bottomed and shallow-a perfect laboratory for pushing boundaries, be it practicing to kite or fine-tuning your bag of tricks. (Lunch and beverages included)
